content:
# Heavy Metals

**Summary**: Metallic elements that can be toxic to humans at high concentrations.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] analyzed heavy-metals in groundwater to assess environmental impact.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] recommended future research on heavy metal concentrations in waste.

- [[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014]] assessed heavy metal concentration levels to evaluate soil health.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] investigated the removal of heavy metals from water.

- [[2015-bello|Bello, 2015]] studied heavy metals (Cd, Pb, Ni) as inorganic pollutants in water.

- [[2015-jawad|Jawad, 2015]] assessed heavy metal contamination in the sediment of a man-made wetland.

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] measured heavy metal levels in the airborne particulate matter to assess environmental risk.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] addressed the removal of heavy metals like Hg2+ from water systems.

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] assessed heavy metal contamination in the mangrove ecosystem.

- [[2022-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2022]] assessed the impact of dumpsites on surrounding soil quality by measuring heavy metal concentration.

- [[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023]] analyzed heavy metals using EF, SPI, and PLI indices to identify metal enrichment levels.

- [[2024-alenazi|Alenazi, 2024]] evaluated the concentration and risk of heavy metals (HMs) in building dust.

## Scientific Context

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] Heavy metals are key indicators of industrial or agricultural contamination.

- [[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012]] recommended future research on heavy metal concentrations in waste.

- [[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014]] assessed heavy metal concentration levels to evaluate soil health.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] investigated the removal of heavy metals from water.

- [[2015-bello|Bello, 2015]] studied heavy metals (Cd, Pb, Ni) as inorganic pollutants in water.

- [[2015-jawad|Jawad, 2015]] assessed heavy metal contamination in the sediment of a man-made wetland.

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] measured heavy metal levels in the airborne particulate matter to assess environmental risk.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] addressed the removal of heavy metals like Hg2+ from water systems.

- [[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019]] assessed heavy metal contamination in the mangrove ecosystem.

- [[2022-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2022]] assessed the impact of dumpsites on surrounding soil quality by measuring heavy metal concentration.

- [[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023]] analyzed heavy metals using EF, SPI, and PLI indices to identify metal enrichment levels.

- [[2024-alenazi|Alenazi, 2024]] evaluated the concentration and risk of heavy metals (HMs) in building dust.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] Chemical analysis of groundwater samples.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The average concentration of manganese (Mn) in shallow ground water was 0.37 ppm, significantly higher than the MCL of 0.05 ppm. ([[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011, p. 90]])

- The study recommends assessing effects of heavy metals associated with drilling wastes. ([[2012-attyah|Attyah, 2012, p. 134]])

- Cd, Cr, Cu, and Zn were found to exceed allowable limits in industrial and residential areas. ([[2014-adedapo|Adedapo, 2014, p. 12]])

- Heavy metals include Ni, Co, Cd, and Cr, which are non-biodegradable and pose significant health risks. ([[2015-musa|Musa, 2015, p. 15]])

- Concentrations of Cd, Pb, and Ni were tested at 5ppm. ([[2015-bello|Bello, 2015, p. 19]])

- All concentration levels of V, Cr, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Mo, Ba, and Pb were within permissible limits. ([[2015-jawad|Jawad, 2015, p. 14]])

- Several metals (Ba, Zn, Cr, V) were found at high or exceeding limits, while others (As, Cd, Pb) remained within safe ranges. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 104]])

- Mercury (II) was identified as a critical contaminant requiring specific detection and removal techniques. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 24]])

- Maximum concentration of Cu was 102.5 mg/kg in bark tissue. ([[2019-alamran|AlAmran, 2019, p. 15]])

- Previous studies in Saudi Arabia showed significant contamination of metals like Cr, Mn, and Zn in areas near industrial zones. ([[2022-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2022, p. 26]])

- The Uqair area was found to have minimal enrichment compared to the more industrially impacted Eco Park site. ([[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023, p. 59]])

- Dammam and Khobar showed higher amounts of HMs than Jeddah and Jubail. ([[2024-alenazi|Alenazi, 2024, p. 19]])
